definition: ALM mandates, risk parity, and mean-variance optimization embed 1998-2021 negative stock-bond correlations. Mandate review cycles of 3-5 years create slow correction. Under persistent positive correlation, 60/40 portfolio risk increases ~50%. UK LDI crisis (2022) was treated as idiosyncratic rather than paradigmatic.
evidence: ALM, risk parity, mean-variance all embed 1998-2021 negative correlations
Mandate review cycles are 3-5 years, creating slow correction
UK LDI crisis (2022) treated as idiosyncratic rather than paradigmatic
~50% risk increase is quantifiable under positive-correlation assumption
25-40% base rate for >5-year positive correlation regime tempers the claim
open_questions: How quickly will institutional mandates adjust correlation assumptions?
What is the persistence probability for the current positive correlation regime?
Could another LDI-style event occur before mandates are updated?
